what_it_does: Records a channel attachment entry that links the design-assets channel to an image asset via a URI target.
observations: YAML frontmatter with no body content
standouts: target uses an image:// URI scheme
concerns: target path contains a space and parentheses, which may be ambiguous or hard to resolve
no filename, MIME type, dimensions, checksum, or alt text provided
image:// scheme is non-standard and its resolver is unspecified
conventions: id prefixed with att- and a short hash
notes: A sparse metadata stub that registers an image attachment in the design-assets channel.
questions: What does the (48) suffix in the target path represent—size, version, or something else?
How is the image:// URI resolved to an actual file or resource?
Who added this attachment and why?
What is the real filename, format, and dimensions of the image?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to be an attachment ledger entry and it does record an image attachment, so purpose and actual content align.
intention_signals: It seems intended to create a discoverable, timestamped record that a specific image asset belongs to the design-assets channel.
novelty: The image:// URI scheme with a parenthetical qualifier in the path is a non-obvious addressing convention.
contribution: Provides a structured, timestamped attachment record that associates a channel with a specific image asset via a URI, supporting asset lookup and provenance.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger entry for the design-assets channel pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (48) with an added timestamp and empty body.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to catalog an image attachment in the design-assets channel for later reference and retrieval.
